action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 17 trained a waveform-domain ResidualAdapter for 5 epochs. Val PESQ started at the frozen pretrained baseline (2.7876) and degraded to 2.4372, triggering early stopping. The pretrained MetricGAN+ still holds the best test PESQ of 3.134. Analysis: All waveform-residual adapter attempts and full fine-tuning attempts have failed because L1/MR-STFT losses do not correlate with PESQ and drive the model away from the PESQ-optimal adversarially-trained point. A fundamentally different inductive bias is needed. I will try a SpectralAdapter that operates on STFT magnitudes: it predicts a real-valued multiplicative mask (initialized to identity/1.0) over the pretrained model's complex spectrogram, using the noisy magnitude as side information. Spectral masking is aligned with the pretrained model's internal mechanism and perceptually smoother than waveform residuals. Because it starts at identity, it cannot catastrophically degrade performance, and early stopping on validation PESQ will catch any overfitting. Decision: 1) Add SpectralAdapter to model.py. 2) Rewrite train.py to freeze the pretrained enhancer, train only the SpectralAdapter with L1 waveform + L1 STFT-magnitude loss. 3) Update predict.py to load and apply the SpectralAdapter correctly. 4) Run a 10-epoch training with full validation each epoch and patience=5. If val PESQ improves over the 2.7876 baseline, submit the adapter checkpoint.
